title: Isla's New Friend
synopsis: Isla moves to a new neighborhood and feels nervous about making friends at the playground. With her mom's encouragement, she learns that friendship starts with a simple hello and discovers that being kind and inclusive helps everyone have more fun together.

pages.text: Isla stood at the edge of the big playground. She had long wavy red hair that danced in the breeze. Her bright green eyes looked around at all the kids playing. They were running, laughing, and having so much fun.

illustration.description: A girl with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes standing at the entrance of a colorful playground filled with diverse children playing on swings and slides under a bright sunny sky
illustration.alt: Isla watching children play at a sunny playground

pages.text: Isla's family had just moved to this neighborhood. Everything was new and different. The houses looked different. The trees looked different. Even the playground looked different from her old one.

illustration.description: A girl with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes looking at a row of colorful houses and a big oak tree near a bright playground with primary colored equipment and white space around

pages.text: "I don't know anyone here," Isla whispered to her mom. Her mom smiled and gave her a warm hug. "That's okay, sweetie. Every friend was once a stranger. You just need to say hello."

illustration.description: A girl with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes receiving a hug from her mom on a park bench with the sunny playground and diverse kids playing in the background
illustration.alt: Isla getting a hug from her mom at the playground

pages.text: But saying hello felt scary. What if the other kids didn't want to play with her? What if they thought she was weird? Isla's tummy felt full of butterflies as she watched the children play tag.

illustration.description: A girl with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes looking worried while diverse children play an energetic game of tag in bright primary colors on the sunny playground
illustration.alt: Isla feeling nervous while watching kids play tag

pages.text: A girl with curly black hair was sitting alone on the swings. She looked sad and wasn't swinging very high. Isla noticed that none of the other kids were talking to her either.

illustration.description: A girl with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes watching another girl with curly black hair sitting alone on bright colored swings in the sunny playground with white space around them
illustration.alt: Isla noticing a girl sitting alone on the swings

pages.text: Isla remembered what her mom said about saying hello. She took a deep breath and walked over to the swings. Her heart was beating fast, but she kept walking anyway.

illustration.description: A girl with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes walking determinedly across the sunny playground toward the swings with diverse kids playing joyfully in the background using primary colors
illustration.alt: Isla walking toward the swings with determination

pages.text: "Hi," Isla said softly as she sat on the swing next to the girl. "I'm Isla. I just moved here. What's your name?" The girl looked up with surprise and then smiled a little. "I'm Maya," she said.

illustration.description: A girl with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes sitting on a bright colored swing next to a girl with curly black hair, both beginning to smile on the sunny playground
illustration.alt: Isla introducing herself to Maya on the swings

pages.text: "I'm new here too," Maya said. "My family moved here last week. I don't know anyone either." Isla felt much better knowing she wasn't the only new kid. "Want to swing together?" Isla asked.

illustration.description: A girl with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes and Maya both smiling and starting to swing higher on the bright playground equipment under the sunny sky with joyful energy
illustration.alt: Isla and Maya beginning to swing together

pages.text: They swung higher and higher, laughing as they tried to touch the clouds with their toes. Soon, other kids noticed how much fun they were having and came over to join them.

illustration.description: A girl with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes swinging high with Maya and diverse children gathering around in the sunny playground with primary colors, showing inclusive friendship activities and laughter
illustration.alt: Isla and Maya swinging high while other kids join them

pages.text: Soon, Isla and Maya were playing with a whole group of kids. They went down the big slide together, played on the monkey bars, and even started a game of hide-and-seek.

illustration.description: A girl with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes playing with diverse children on colorful slides and monkey bars in the bright sunny playground with primary colors and white space, showing energetic friendship activities
illustration.alt: Isla playing with a group of diverse children on playground equipment

pages.text: When it was time to go home, Isla didn't want to leave. She had made so many new friends! "Can we play again tomorrow?" Maya asked. "Yes!" said Isla. "Let's meet at the swings!"

illustration.description: A girl with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes waving goodbye to Maya and other diverse children at the sunny playground with joyful expressions and bright primary colors
illustration.alt: Isla saying goodbye to her new friends at the playground

pages.text: Walking home with her mom, Isla felt so happy. Her mom asked, "How did you make friends so quickly?" Isla thought about it. "I just said hello," she said. "And I was nice."

illustration.description: A girl with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes walking hand-in-hand with her mom away from the colorful playground on a sunny day with bright colors and white space
illustration.alt: Isla walking home happily with her mom

pages.text: Her mom smiled proudly. "That's exactly right, sweetie. Being kind and friendly is the best way to make friends. I'm proud of you for being brave and including Maya." Isla couldn't wait to play again tomorrow.

illustration.description: A girl with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes smiling brightly at her mom in front of their new house with the sunny playground visible in the background, showing joyful energy and primary colors
illustration.alt: Isla smiling proudly with her mom in front of their new home

metadata.illustrationStyle: bright sunny playground, diverse kids playing, primary colors, white space, joyful energetic, inclusive, park swings slides, laughter, friendship activities

questions.question: Why was Isla nervous at the playground?
choices: She was new and didn't know anyone
She was afraid of the swings
questions.explanation: Isla was nervous because her family had just moved to a new neighborhood and she didn't know any of the children at the playground.

questions.question: Who did Isla meet first at the playground?
questions.explanation: Isla first met Maya, who was sitting alone on the swings and was also new to the neighborhood.

questions.question: What did Isla's mom tell her about making friends?
choices: Every friend was once a stranger, just say hello
Wait for others to talk to you first
Only play with kids you already know
questions.explanation: Isla's mom encouraged her by saying 'Every friend was once a stranger. You just need to say hello.'

questions.question: How did Maya and Isla discover they had something in common?
choices: They were both new to the neighborhood
They both liked the same games
questions.explanation: Both Isla and Maya had recently moved to the neighborhood, which made them feel connected and less alone.

questions.question: What happened after Isla and Maya started playing together?
choices: Other kids joined them and they all became friends
questions.explanation: When other children saw how much fun Isla and Maya were having, they came over to join them, and soon they were all playing together.
